zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Alice White於1539年出生在英國薩默塞特奧特福德時，她的父親Richard White20歲，母親Hellen Kirton16歲。她於1565年7月10日與John Abcomb在英國英格蘭薩默塞特奧特福德結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1623年5月25日在英國英格蘭薩默塞特奧特福德享年84歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭薩默塞特奧特福德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Alice White于1539年出生在英国薩默塞特奧特福德时，她的父亲Richard White20岁，母亲Hellen Kirton16岁。她于1565年7月10日与John Abcomb在英国英格兰薩默塞特奧特福德结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1623年5月25日在英国英格兰薩默塞特奧特福德享年84岁，埋葬在英国英格兰薩默塞特奧特福德。

nameMeanings.name: White
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English white, wit (Old English hwīt ‘white’), hence a nickname for someone with white hair or a pale complexion. In some cases it is perhaps from a Middle English survival of the Old English personal name Hwīta, a short form of names in Hwīt- (from hwīt ‘white’). The name may also be topographic, referring to someone who lived by a bend or curve in a river or road (from Old English wiht ‘bend’), the source of the placename of Great Whyte in Ramsey, Huntingdonshire (compare Wight ). This name is also a variant of Wight . The surname White is also very common among African Americans.

Irish and Scottish: adopted for any of several Irish and Scottish Gaelic names based on bán ‘white, fair’ (see Bain 1, McElwain ) or fionn ‘fair’ (see Finn 1). The English surname has been Gaelicized in Ireland as de Faoite.

Americanized form (translation into English) of various European surnames meaning ‘white’, for example German Weiss , French Blanc , Polish Białas (see Bialas ), Slovenian Belec , or any other synonymous Slavic surname beginning with Bel-, Bev-, Biel- or Bil-.

variants.name: Whitty
meanings: topographic name denoting a ‘dweller by the white hedge or the white (forest) enclosure’, from Middle English whit ‘white’ (Old English hwīt) + heie ‘hedge, enclosure’ (Old English (ge)hæg and Old French haie) perhaps in reference to whitethorn (hawthorn) hedging. This English name has been recorded in Ireland (Wexford) since the 13th century. It has been Gaelicized as de Faoite, see also White .
nickname from Middle English whit ‘white’ + eie ‘eye’ (Old English hwīt + ēage). Compare Blackie 2 and Greeney .
variant of   Witty  , with which this surname may have been confused.

variants.name: Whitson
meanings: English and Scottish (Midlothian): from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Whiteson, literally ‘white son’ or perhaps ‘son of someone named White ’. Occasionally, a variant of Whiston .

variants.name: Whitt
meanings: English (Nottinghamshire): variant of   White  .
Possibly also an Americanized form of German   Witt  .

variants.name: McQuitty
meanings: Irish (northern): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Fhaoitigh, a patronymic from a Gaelicization of English White . See also McWhite .

variants.name: Whitecotton
meanings: Apparently a compound of   White   +   Cotton  , but the history is obscure.

variants.name: McElwain
meanings: Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Giolla Bháin (Irish) or Mac Gille Bháin (Scottish) ‘son of the fair-haired lad’, often translated into English as White or Whyte but otherwise indistinguishable, once Anglicized, from Scottish Gaelic Mac Gille Bheathain in 2 below. Compare Kilbane .
Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Gille Bheathain ‘son of the servant (i.e. devotee) of (Saint) Beathan’, a personal name representing a diminutive of beatha ‘life’. Compare McBain .

variants.name: McWhite
meanings: Irish: partly translated Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Faoitigh, a patronymic from a Gaelic form of English White .

variants.name: Wight
meanings: Scottish and English: nickname from Middle English wiht, wight ‘agile, nimble, strong, brave’ (Old Norse víg with the Old Norse neuter suffix -t, for which compare the word scant), or from the corresponding Older Scots word wicht. Possibly sometimes a nickname from Middle English wight ‘living creature; small portion or thing’, or from the corresponding Older Scots word wicht.

English: habitational name from the Isle of Wight. The island is recorded as Vectis (its Latin name) c. 150 AD . Its name derives from a British word, perhaps related to Welsh gwaith ‘turn, course’, meaning ‘place of the division’, in reference to the island's position in the Solent.
English and Scottish: variant of   White  .

variants.name: Whites
meanings: English: variant of White with genitive or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Whitman
meanings: English (southern): from the Middle English personal name Whytman (Old English Hwītmann, from hwīt ‘white’ + mann ‘man’). Compare White 1 and also Wightman , with which this surname was probably confused.
History: John Whitman settled in Weymouth, MA, c. 1638. The poet Walt Whitman (1819–92) was descended from Joseph Whitman, who had settled in Stratford, CT, from England around 1660.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

variants.name: Alys
meanings: Variant spelling of   Alice .

variants.name: Alis
meanings: equivalent of Alice .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Alice White was born in 1539, in Otterford, Somerset, England, her father, Richard White, was 20 and her mother, Hellen Kirton, was 16. She married John Abcomb on 10 July 1565, in Otterford, Somerset, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 5 daughters. She died on 25 May 1623, in Otterford, Somerset, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 84, and was buried in Otterford, Somerset, England, United Kingdom.

Elle a épousé John Abcomb le 10 juillet 1565, à Otterford, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée le 25 mai 1623, à Otterford, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 84 ans, et a été enterrée à Otterford, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Alice Whiteが1539年にイギリスソメルセトオッテルフォードで生まれた時，父Richard Whiteは20歳，母Hellen Kirtonは16歳であった。1565年7月10日にイギリスイギリスソメルセトオッテルフォードでJohn Abcombと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1623年5月25日にイギリスイギリスソメルセトオッテルフォードで84歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスソメルセトオッテルフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Alice White(이)가 1539년 영국 서머세트 오터퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard White(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Hellen Kirton(은)는 16세였다. 그녀는 1565년 7월 10일 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 오터퍼드에서 John Abcomb(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1623년 5월 25일 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 오터퍼드에서 84세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 오터퍼드에 묻혔다.

Ela faleceu em 25 de maio de 1623, em Otterford, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 84 anos, e foi sepultada em Otterford, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Alice White родилась в 1539 г. в местности оттирфорд, сомирсет, Англия, её отцу по имени Richard White было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Hellen Kirton, 16. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Abcomb 10 июля 1565 г. в местности оттирфорд, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Она умерла 25 мая 1623 г. в местности оттирфорд, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 84 лет и была похоронена в местности оттирфорд, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания.

es.Saint Neot, Cornwall, England: St Neot, Cornwall, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Alice White nació en 1539, en Otterford, Somerset, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard White, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Hellen Kirton, tenía 16 años. Se casó con John Abcomb el 10 de julio de 1565, en Otterford, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 25 de mayo de 1623, en Otterford, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 84 años, y fue sepultada en Otterford, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
